result:
All the unreachable warnings are pre-existing in other files, not from my changes. Build is clean. Done.

## Report

**Root cause** — `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs::parse` (lines 2732–2798) calls `mainfunc → statlist → primaryexp` while the lexer is never wired in. Every `// TODO(port): lua_lex::next(ls, state)?;` site (including the critical one in `mainfunc` at line 2713 that should read the first token) is unimplemented, so `lexstate.t.token` stays at its default value `0`. `primaryexp` (line 1791) sees `0` and emits the spurious "unexpected symbol" at line 1805. The local `LexState` in `lua-parse` is a Phase-A stub, distinct from the canonical `lua_lex::LexState`, and `lua-parse` doesn't even depend on `lua-lex` yet — the bridge is the documented Phase-B reconcile in the PORT STATUS trailer.

**Fix** — two targeted edits:

1. `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs` (parse): short-circuit before `mainfunc` with a `phase-b:` syntax-error sentinel naming the missing bridge. This stops the half-implemented path from emitting fake parser diagnostics.
2. `crates/lua-cli/src/main.rs`: when `load_string` returns a non-zero status, render the on-stack message with a `Syntax: ` tag prefix (load failures are always syntax errors in Lua), so the implement-loop classifier regex `(Runtime|Syntax|Error): phase-[a-z0-9_-]+:` recognizes phase-b sentinels surfaced via load.

**New error** — `[err] Syntax: phase-b: lua_parse::parse needs lua_lex bridge (mainfunc requires lua_lex::next to drive tokens; local LexState stub vs lua_lex::LexState reconcile) (load_string status=1)`. The original "unexpected symbol" is gone, and the loop now classifies this as a `stub`, routing it to the implement agent to do the lua-lex ↔ lua-parse wiring.
